name: Nikita Context Protocol context pack
summary: A source-backed preparation layer for talking with Nikita Kramar about his public writing on AI agents, context architecture, open web memory, orchestration, tool mastery, and embodied life.
corePremise: Agents may read this context. They may not pretend to be Nikita.
bestUse: generate candidate experiments to ask Nikita about
highConfidenceObservations: Nikita explicitly defends the Open Web and welcomes synthetic readers on his public site.
He frames AI agent success as a function of context, rules, documentation, tools, access, and token spend.
He predicts a shift from manual coding toward orchestration and human approval.
He treats agent-tool mastery as a distinct skill in his Hidden Third Variable essay.
His Now page says he is comparing DSPy, LangGraph, and LangChain while testing the one-man billion-dollar company idea.
workingInferences: A personal site can become useful agent context without becoming a person simulator.
The unresolved tension is what human judgment and embodied life become when agent execution gets cheap.
The best artifact to show him is a small working protocol, not a broad pitch deck.
suggestedOpeners: Your site reads like it already wants an agent-readable layer, but not a chatbot that pretends to be you. I tried to make the smallest useful version. Does this match what you meant by keeping the web open for synthetic readers?
WWNKD, as a joke but also as a constraint: don't ask what Nikita would say; ask what his public primitives suggest testing next.
Your hidden-third-variable essay made me think tool mastery needs a failure-analysis artifact. What should the artifact after a failed agent run be?
